brief_justification: Paper A concerns geometric mirror symmetry on Calabi-Yau 3-folds — hyperkahler intermediate Jacobians, Rozansky-Witten theories, SYZ transforms, and derived categories with stability conditions — whereas paper B derives a two-term policy gradient for reinforcement-learning training of masked diffusion language models. The 'mirror'/'diffusion' overlap is purely lexical: there is no common governing equation, transition, or transferable machinery, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
